purpose: Map high-risk Blockless pitch claims to the exact benchmark, market, and source gates required before the claim can be used.

claims.tempting_language: Blockless is the only closed-loop AI hardware builder.
claims.safe_language_today: Closed loop is contested; Blockless is testing whether verified recipes preserve more rerun-critical context.
claims.minimum_pass_condition: Blockless wins task success, artifact completeness, repair quality, and second-user rerun against the closest same-task baselines.
kill_conditions: PleaseDontCode produces an equally rerunnable artifact.
Agentic IDE/CLI plus PlatformIO solves the same loop with similar friction.
Blockless output omits wiring, package, flash, log, repair, or rerun context.

claims.safe_language_today: Tutorials are the baseline to beat; speed is unproven until same-task timing exists.
claims.minimum_pass_condition: Median sentence-to-working-device time is lower while preserving equal or better correctness and rerun context.
kill_conditions: Tutorial has lower elapsed time.
Tutorial produces clearer understanding or lower support burden.
Blockless feels faster but requires more hidden repair.
forbidden_inferences: One successful demo proves lower friction.

claims.tempting_language: Blockless recipes are reusable and rerunnable.
claims.safe_language_today: Verified recipe is the intended artifact; rerun quality must be measured by a second tester.
claims.minimum_pass_condition: A second tester reruns the artifact without hidden session context and with fewer undocumented interventions than tutorial/project-gallery baselines.
kill_conditions: Rerun requires original creator help.
Rerun depends on local state or undocumented credentials.
Tutorial completion is easier or more trusted.

claims.tempting_language: Blockless is building the Hardware App Store.
claims.safe_language_today: The near-term object is a verified recipe registry.
claims.minimum_pass_condition: At least 20 verified recipes, 30 percent second-user rerun, 15 percent fork/edit, and observed paid/kit/workspace commitment behavior.
kill_conditions: Recipes are browsed but not rerun.
Users prefer tutorials because they explain context better.
No paid, kit, workspace, or support commitment appears.
forbidden_inferences: Community project galleries prove marketplace liquidity.
Software appstore behavior transfers to hardware.

claims.tempting_language: Blockless is Cursor or Lovable for hardware.
claims.safe_language_today: Software vibe coding shows workflow shape; hardware needs separate proof because the artifact is physical.
claims.minimum_pass_condition: Users start follow-up hardware projects, rerun/fork recipes, and take concrete paid or commitment steps with support load low enough to scale.
kill_conditions: Users like the demo but do not return.
Users cannot inspect or trust the hardware artifact.
Paid interest appears only after founder support.
forbidden_inferences: Software project creation proves hardware completion.

claims.tempting_language: MicroPython is 10x better for hardware creation.
claims.safe_language_today: MicroPython is a plausible v1 wedge; runtime advantage is benchmark-gated.
claims.minimum_pass_condition: MicroPython-based Blockless runs show lower total friction and repair burden than Arduino/C, declarative, JS/TS, and live/visual alternatives on the same task class.
kill_conditions: Arduino/PlatformIO solves with similar reliability.
ESPHome/Tasmota wins on automation tasks.
MicroBlocks/XOD provide clearer live iteration for target users.
forbidden_inferences: Hot reload alone proves product superiority.

claims.tempting_language: Vendors do not have Blockless's hardware context.
claims.safe_language_today: Vendors own static hardware truth; Blockless must prove dynamic execution history compounds.
claims.minimum_pass_condition: Blockless preserves measured run, repair, and rerun context that official tools/docs/AI do not, and that context improves future success.
kill_conditions: Official docs plus generic agent produce equivalent project.
ESP-Claw/MCP/RainMaker solves enough of the workflow.
Blockless data is mostly normalized vendor documentation.
forbidden_inferences: Vendor context is stale by default.

claims.tempting_language: Blockless owns sensor events, dashboards, and automations.
claims.safe_language_today: Blockless may export endpoint/device context; IoT app/control value is contested.
claims.minimum_pass_condition: Blockless preserves more rerun-critical device and endpoint context than dashboard/YAML/cloud/flow exports while interoperating with stronger IoT platforms.
kill_conditions: Existing IoT platform solves workflow faster.
Recurring value belongs to cloud/dashboard platform.
Blockless output is only a dashboard wrapper.
forbidden_inferences: OTA/device cloud value belongs to Blockless by default.

claims.tempting_language: Blockless owns production IoT, robotics, or industrial AI workflows.
claims.safe_language_today: Blockless starts upstream at bench recipe creation; production lifecycle value belongs to separate baselines until measured.
claims.minimum_pass_condition: Blockless demonstrably improves physical recipe bring-up before clean handoff to lifecycle platforms, or beats them on a task they naturally fit.
kill_conditions: User's job is fleet/device/cloud operation, not recipe generation.
Industrial app-enablement platform owns dashboards/workflows/analytics.
Robotics platform owns machine APIs, model deployment, and fleet control.
forbidden_inferences: Prototype recipe value equals production IoT value.
Industrial AI demand proves maker recipe demand.
Robotics lifecycle is the same market as ESP32 bench recipes.

claims.tempting_language: Blockless is the best no-code hardware tool for education.
claims.safe_language_today: Education is a possible wedge only if physical recipe completion beats blocks and simulation with acceptable teacher support.
claims.minimum_pass_condition: Students complete more reliably than blocks/simulation and can explain wiring and logic with equal or lower teacher intervention.
kill_conditions: Simulation/blocks produce higher completion.
Agent output is opaque to students.
Physical setup dominates the class session.
forbidden_inferences: One teacher compliment proves education demand.

claims.tempting_language: Blockless can be a $100M ARR hardware appstore because Arduino/Raspberry Pi/Cursor markets are huge.
claims.safe_language_today: Market size must be bottom-up from measured builders, repeat use, reruns, paid units, support cost, and retention.
claims.minimum_pass_condition: A bottom-up model ties visitor-to-first-build, second-build, rerun, fork, kit, workspace, paid recipe, churn, gross margin, and support burden to measured cohorts.
kill_conditions: Model depends on MCU shipments or installed base instead of active builder conversion.
No repeat-use or paid behavior exists.
Support or recipe-maintenance burden consumes the margin.
forbidden_inferences: Arduino/Raspberry Pi installed base proves Blockless demand.
